title: Security, Privacy, Reliability, and Layered Quality Evidence · Decision Evidence Lens: Privacy Lifecycle
summary: A product passes a familiar unit test but trusts input, overcollects data, leaks failure details, loses work, and has no threat or recovery evidence. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 privacy lifecycle increment in trust and quality assurance case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Privacy Lifecycle · Theory Privacy Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Privacy Lifecycle contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Privacy Lifecycle · Theory Privacy Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk. For Predict Privacy Lifecycle · Theory Privacy Lifecycle, keep Privacy Lifecycle connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

steps.title: Bound Privacy Lifecycle · Theory Privacy Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Privacy Lifecycle application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

Evidence target: Bound Privacy Lifecycle · Theory Privacy Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk. For Bound Privacy Lifecycle · Theory Privacy Lifecycle, keep Privacy Lifecycle connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Privacy Lifecycle while producing trust and quality assurance case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The worked example variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk. A defensible solution applies Privacy Lifecycle, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to trust and quality assurance case.

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp1-privacy-lifecycle — Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
focus: pp1-privacy-lifecycle
decision-evidence: Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Defend the Privacy Lifecycle model · Theory Privacy Lifecycle

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
